1: An item that prevents weak wild Pokémon from appearing for 100 steps after its use.
2: An item that prevents weak wild Pokémon from appearing for 200 steps after its use.
3: An item that prevents weak wild Pokémon from appearing for 250 steps after its use.
4: A black flute made from blown glass. Its melody makes wild Pokémon less likely to appear.
5: A white flute made from blown glass. Its melody makes wild Pokémon more likely to appear.
6: A sweet honey with an aroma that attracts wild Pokémon when used in grass, caves or on special trees.
7: A long, durable rope. Use it to escape instantly from a cave or a dungeon.
8: A small red shard. It appears to be from some sort of implement made long ago.
9: A small blue shard. It appears to be from some sort of implement made long ago.
10: A small yellow shard. It appears to be from some sort of implement made long ago.
11: A small green shard. It appears to be from some sort of implement made long ago.
12: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It is colored orange.
13: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It is a clear, light blue.
14: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It has a thunderbolt pattern.
15: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It has a leaf pattern.
16: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It is as black as the night sky.
17: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It is as red as the sun.
18: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It shines with a dazzling light.
19: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It is as dark as dark can be.
20: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It sparkles like eyes.
21: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It is shaped like an egg.
22: A fossil of an ancient Pokémon that lived in the sea. It appears to be part of a seashell.
23: A fossil of an ancient Pokémon that lived in the sea. It appears to be part of a shell.
24: A piece of clear amber that contains the genetic material of an ancient Pokémon.
25: A fossil of an ancient Pokémon that lived in the sea. It appears to be part of a plant root.
26: A fossil of an ancient Pokémon that lived in the sea. It appears to be part of a claw.
27: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that lived on the land. It appears to be part of a collar.
28: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that lived on the land. It appears to be part of a head.
29: A small and rare mushroom. It is quite popular among certain maniacal fan segments.
30: A large and rare mushroom. It is very popular among certain maniacal fan segments.
31: A somewhat-small pearl that sparkles in a pretty silver color. It can be sold cheaply to shops.
32: A quite-large pearl that sparkles in a pretty silver color. It can be sold at a high price to shops.
33: Lovely, red-colored sand with a loose, silky feel. It can be sold at a high price to shops.
34: A shard of a pretty gem that sparkles in a red color. It can be sold at a high price to shops.
35: A nugget of pure gold that gives off a lustrous gleam. It can be sold at a high price to shops.
36: A pretty, heart-shaped scale that is extremely rare. It glows faintly in the colors of the rainbow.
37: A bone that is extremely valuable for Pokémon archaeology. It can be sold for a high price to shops.
38: Pure salt that was discovered deep inside the Shoal Cave. It is extremely salty.
39: A pretty seashell that was found deep inside the Shoal Cave. It is striped in blue and white.
40: A vital item that is needed to keep a stone tower from collapsing. Voices can be heard from it occasionally.
41: A plant fertilizer. It speeds up the growth of Berries. However, it also dries the soil faster.
42: A plant fertilizer. It slows the growth of Berries and causes the soil to dry slower.
43: A plant fertilizer. It extends the time ripened Berries remain on their plants before falling.
44: A plant fertilizer. It ups the number of times new plants grow where mature plants withered.
45: A red Apricorn. It assails your nostrils.
46: A yellow Apricorn. It has an invigorating scent.
47: A blue Apricorn. It smells a bit like grass.
48: A green Apricorn. It has a mysterious, aromatic scent.
49: A pink Apricorn. It has a nice, sweet scent.
50: A white Apricorn. It doesn't smell like anything.
51: A black Apricorn. It has an indescribable scent.
52: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It casts a tricky glare that lowers the opponent's accuracy.
53: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It restores any lowered stat in battle. It can be used only once.
54: An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder gets a share of a battle's Exp. Points without battling.
55: An item to be held by a Pokémon. A light, sharp claw that lets the bearer move first occasionally.
56: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a bell with a comforting chime that calms the holder.
57: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It snaps the holder out of infatuation, just once.
58: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It may cause the foe to flinch when the holder inflicts damage.
59: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a shiny, silver powder that ups the power of Bug-type moves.
60: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It doubles a battle's prize money if the holding Pokémon joins in.
61: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It helps keep wild Pokémon away if the holder is the first one in the party.
62: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It enables the holder to flee from any wild Pokémon without fail.
63: An item to be held by a Pokémon. The Pokémon holding this peculiar stone is prevented from evolving.
64: An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder may endure a potential KO attack, leaving it with just 1 HP.
65: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is an egg filled with happiness that earns extra Exp. Points in battle.
66: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a lens that boosts the holder's critical-hit ratio.
67: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a magnifying lens that slightly boosts the accuracy of moves.
68: An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder moves after the foe, its critical-hit ratio will be boosted.
69: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a special metallic film that ups the power of Steel-type moves.
70: An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder's HP is gradually restored during battle.
71: A thick and tough scale. Dragon-type Pokémon may be holding this item when caught.
72: An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder's HP is restored a little every time it inflicts damage.
73: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a headband that slightly boosts the power of physical moves.
74: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a thick pair of glasses that boosts the power of special moves.
75: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a well-worn belt that boosts the power of supereffective moves.
76: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts the power of moves, but at the cost of some HP on each hit.
77: A single-use item to be held by a Pokémon. It allows the immediate use of a move that charges.
78: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a bizarre orb that badly poisons the holder in battle.
79: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a bizarre orb that inflicts a burn on the holder in battle.
80: An item to be held by a Pokémon. If it has full HP, the holder will endure a potential KO attack.
81: A Pokémon hold item that boosts a move used consecutively.
82: A Pokémon hold item that cuts Speed. It makes airborne holders susceptible to Ground moves.
83: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is tremendously heavy and makes the holder move slower than usual.
84: A long, bright red string to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder becomes infatuated, the foe does too.
85: A hold item that gradually restores the HP of Poison-type Pokémon. It inflicts damage on all other types.
86: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of multiturn attacks like Bind and Wrap.
87: A hold item that damages the holder on every turn. It may latch on to foes that touch the holder.
88: A tough, discarded carapace to be held by a Pokémon. It enables the holder to switch out of battle.
89: A Pokémon hold item that boosts the power of HP-stealing moves to let the holder recover more HP.
90: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This headband ups Attack, but allows the use of only one kind of move.
91: A Pokémon hold item. These distinctive glasses boost Sp. Atk, but allow only one move to be used.
92: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This scarf boosts Speed, but allows the use of only one kind of move.
93: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of barrier moves like Light Screen and Reflect.
94: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of the move Hail used by the holder.
95: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of the move Sandstorm used by the holder.
96: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of the move Sunny Day used by the holder.
97: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of the move Rain Dance used by the holder.

157: A transparent device filled with all sorts of data. It was produced by Silph Co.

170: A spray-type medicine for wounds. It restores the HP of one Pokémon by just 20 points.
171: A spray-type medicine for wounds. It restores the HP of one Pokémon by 50 points.
172: A spray-type medicine for wounds. It restores the HP of one Pokémon by 200 points.
173: A spray-type medicine for wounds. It completely restores the HP of a single Pokémon.
174: A medicine that fully restores the HP and heals any status problems of a single Pokémon.
175: It revives all fainted Pokémon. In doing so, it also fully restores their HP.
176: A spray-type medicine. It lifts the effect of poison from one Pokémon.
177: A spray-type medicine. It heals a single Pokémon that is suffering from a burn.
178: A spray-type medicine. It defrosts a Pokémon that has been frozen solid.
179: A spray-type medicine. It awakens a Pokémon from the clutches of sleep.
180: A spray-type medicine. It eliminates paralysis from a single Pokémon.
181: A spray-type medicine. It heals all the status problems of a single Pokémon.
182: Lavaridge Town's local specialty. It heals all the status problems of one Pokémon.
183: Old Chateau's hidden specialty. It heals all the status problems of a single Pokémon.
184: A medicine that revives a fainted Pokémon. It restores half the Pokémon's maximum HP.
185: A medicine that revives a fainted Pokémon. It fully restores the Pokémon's HP.
186: A 100% pure juice made of Berries. It restores the HP of one Pokémon by just 20 points.
187: Water with a high mineral content. It restores the HP of one Pokémon by 50 points.
188: A fizzy soda drink. It restores the HP of one Pokémon by 60 points.
189: A very sweet drink. It restores the HP of one Pokémon by 80 points.
190: Milk with a very high nutrition content. It restores the HP of one Pokémon by 100 points.
191: A very bitter medicine powder. It restores the HP of one Pokémon by 50 points.
192: A very bitter root. It restores the HP of one Pokémon by 200 points.
193: A very bitter medicine powder. It heals all the status problems of a single Pokémon.
194: A very bitter medicinal herb. It revives a fainted Pokémon, fully restoring its HP.
195: It restores the PP of a Pokémon's selected move by a maximum of 10 points.
196: It fully restores the PP of a single selected move that has been learned by the target Pokémon.
197: It restores the PP of all the moves learned by the targeted Pokémon by 10 points each.
198: It fully restores the PP of all the moves learned by the targeted Pokémon.
199: A nutritious drink for Pokémon. It raises the base HP of a single Pokémon.
200: A nutritious drink for Pokémon. It raises the base Attack stat of a single Pokémon.
201: A nutritious drink for Pokémon. It raises the base Defense stat of a single Pokémon.
202: A nutritious drink for Pokémon. It raises the base Speed stat of a single Pokémon.
203: A nutritious drink for Pokémon. It raises the base Sp. Atk (Special Attack) stat of a single Pokémon.
204: A nutritious drink for Pokémon. It raises the base Sp. Def (Special Defense) stat of a single Pokémon.
205: It slightly raises the maximum PP of a selected move that has been learned by the target Pokémon.
206: It maximally raises the top PP of a selected move that has been learned by the target Pokémon.
207: A candy that is packed with energy. It raises the level of a single Pokémon by one.
208: The best Ball with the ultimate level of performance. It will catch any wild Pokémon without fail.
209: An ultra-performance Ball that provides a higher Pokémon catch rate than a Great Ball.
210: A good, high-performance Ball that provides a higher Pokémon catch rate than a standard Poké Ball.
211: A device for catching wild Pokémon. A capsule system that is thrown like a ball at the target.
212: A special Poké Ball that is used only in the Great Marsh. It is decorated in a camouflage pattern.
213: A somewhat different Poké Ball that works especially well on Water- and Bug-type Pokémon.
214: A somewhat different Poké Ball that works especially well on Pokémon that live in the sea.
215: A somewhat different Poké Ball that works especially well on weaker Pokémon in the wild.
216: A somewhat different Poké Ball that works especially well on Pokémon species that were previously caught.
217: A somewhat different Ball that becomes progressively better the more turns there are in a battle.
218: A comfortable Poké Ball that makes a caught wild Pokémon quickly grow friendly.
219: A somewhat rare Poké Ball that has been specially made to commemorate an event of some sort.
220: A somewhat different Poké Ball that can easily catch wild Pokémon at night or in dark places.
221: A remedial Poké Ball that restores the caught Pokémon's HP and eliminates any status problem.
222: A somewhat different Poké Ball that works better at the start of a wild encounter.
223: A quite rare Poké Ball that has been specially crafted to commemorate an occasion of some sort.
224: A Poké Ball that makes it easier to catch Pokémon which are quick to run away.
225: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon that are a lower level than your own.
226: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon hooked by a Rod when fishing.
227: A Poké Ball for catching very heavy Pokémon.
228: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon that are the opposite gender of your Pokémon.
229: A Poké Ball that makes caught Pokémon more friendly.
230: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon that evolve using the Moon Stone.
231: A special Poké Ball for the Bug-Catching Contest.
232: A special Poké Ball for the Pal Park.
233: The user focuses its mind before launching this attack. It will fail if the user is hit before it is used.
234: Sharp, huge claws hook and slash the foe quickly and with great power.
235: The foe is hit with a pulsing blast of water. It may also confuse the target.
236: The user quietly focuses its mind and calms its spirit to raise its Sp. Atk and Sp. Def stats.
237: The foe is scared off, to be replaced by another Pokémon in its party. In the wild, the battle ends.
238: A move that leaves the foe badly poisoned. This poison damage worsens every turn.
239: Summons a hailstorm that lasts for five turns. The hailstorm damages all types except Ice.
240: The user tenses its muscles to bulk up its body, boosting both its Attack and Defense stats.
241: The user forcefully shoots seeds at the foe. Two to five seeds are shot in rapid succession.
242: A variable move that changes type and power depending on the hidden ability of the Pokémon using it.
243: The user intensifies the sun for five turns, powering up Fire-type moves.
244: The target is taunted into a rage that allows it to use only attack moves for three turns.
245: The foe is struck with an icy-cold beam of energy. It may also freeze the target solid.
246: A howling blizzard is summoned to strike the foe. It may also freeze the target solid.
247: The foe is attacked with a powerful beam. The user must rest on the next turn to regain its energy.
248: A wondrous wall of light is put up to suppress damage from special attacks for five turns.
249: It enables the user to evade all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.
250: The user summons a heavy rain that falls for five turns, powering up Water-type moves.
251: A nutrient-draining attack. The user's HP is restored by half the damage taken by the target.
252: The user creates a protective field that prevents status problems like poison, paralysis, burn, and sleep.
253: A full-power attack that grows more powerful the less the user likes its Trainer.
254: A two-turn attack. The user gathers light, then blasts a bundled beam on the second turn.
255: The foe is slammed with a sturdy tail of steel. It may also lower the target's Defense stat if it hits.
256: A strong electric blast is loosed at the foe. It may also leave the foe paralyzed.
257: A wicked thunderbolt is dropped on the foe to inflict damage. It may also leave the target paralyzed.
258: The user sets off an earthquake that hits all the Pokémon in the battle.
259: A full-power attack that grows more powerful the more the user likes its Trainer.
260: The user burrows, then attacks on the second turn. It can also be used to exit dungeons.
261: The foe is hit by a strong telekinetic force. It may also reduce the foe's Sp. Def stat.
262: The user hurls a shadowy blob at the foe. It may also lower the foe's Sp. Def stat.
263: The user attacks with tough fists, etc. It can also break any barrier such as Light Screen and Reflect.
264: The user begins moving so quickly that it creates illusory copies to raise its evasiveness.
265: A wondrous wall of light is put up to suppress damage from physical attacks for five turns.
266: The user strikes the foe with a quick jolt of electricity. This attack cannot be evaded.
267: The foe is scorched with an intense blast of fire. The target may also be left with a burn.
268: Unsanitary sludge is hurled at the foe. It may also poison the target.
269: A five-turn sandstorm is summoned to hurt all combatants except the Rock, Ground, and Steel types.
270: The foe is attacked with an intense blast of all-consuming fire. It may also leave the target with a burn.
271: Large boulders are hurled at the foe to inflict damage. It may also make the target flinch.
272: The user confounds the foe with speed, then strikes. The attack lands without fail.
273: The user torments and enrages the foe, making it incapable of using the same move twice in a row.
274: An attack move that doubles its power if the user is poisoned, paralyzed, or has a burn.
275: An attack move with effects that vary depending on the user's environment.
276: The user goes to sleep for two turns. It fully restores the user's HP and heals any status problem.
277: If it is the opposite gender of the user, the foe becomes infatuated and less likely to attack.
278: The user attacks and steals the foe's held item simultaneously. It can't steal if the user holds an item.
279: The foe is hit with wings of steel. It may also raise the user's Defense stat.
280: The user employs its psychic power to exchange abilities with the foe.
281: The user steals the effects of any healing or status-changing move the foe attempts to use.
282: The user attacks the foe at full power using fiery energy. It also sharply reduces the user's Sp. Atk.
283: The user lands and rests its body. It restores the user's HP by up to half of its top HP.
284: The user attacks at full power. It may also lower the foe's Sp. Def stat if it hits.
285: The user draws power from nature and fires it at the foe. It may also lower the target's Sp. Def.
286: A restrained attack that prevents the foe from fainting. The target is left with at least 1 HP.
287: If the foe's HP is down to under half, this attack will hit with double the power.
288: The user flings its held item at the foe to attack. Its power and effects depend on the item.
289: The user attacks with an electric charge. The user uses the remaining electricity to up its Sp. Atk.
290: The user endures any attack, leaving 1 HP. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.
291: The foe is attacked with a shock wave generated by the user's gaping mouth.
292: An energy-draining punch. The user's HP is restored by half the damage taken by the target.
293: The user shoots a sinister, bluish white flame at the foe to inflict a burn.
294: The foe is attacked with powdery scales blown by wind. It may also raise all the user's stats.
295: It prevents the foe from using its held item. Its Trainer is also prevented from using items on it.
296: The user explodes to inflict damage on all Pokémon in battle. The user faints upon using this move.
297: The user slashes with a sharp claw made from shadows. It has a high critical-hit ratio.
298: The user stores power, then attacks. Its power is doubled if used after the foe.
299: The user recycles a single-use item that has been used in battle so it can be used again.
300: The user charges the foe using every bit of its power. It must rest on the next turn to recover.
301: The user polishes its body to reduce drag. It sharply raises the Speed stat.
302: The user flashes a light that cuts the foe's accuracy. It can also be used to illuminate caves.
303: The user stabs the foe with a sharpened stone from below. It has a high critical-hit ratio.
304: An attack move that inflicts double the damage if the user has been hurt by the foe in the same turn.
305: A weak electric charge is launched at the foe. It causes paralysis if it hits.
306: The user tackles the foe from a high-speed spin. The slower the user, the greater the damage.
307: A frenetic dance to uplift the fighting spirit. It sharply raises the user's Attack stat.
308: The user lays a trap of levitating stones around the foe. The trap hurts foes that switch into battle.
309: The user hypnotizes itself into copying any stat change made by the foe.
310: If it is the opposite gender of the user, the foe is charmed into sharply lowering its Sp. Atk stat.
311: The user releases a horrible aura imbued with dark thoughts. It may also make the target flinch.
312: Large boulders are hurled at the foe to inflict damage. It may also make the target flinch.
313: The user slashes at the foe by crossing its scythes or claws as if they were a pair of scissors.
314: While it is asleep, the user randomly uses one of the moves it knows.
315: The user draws power from the Berry it is holding to attack. The Berry determines its type and power.
316: The foe is stabbed with a tentacle or arm seeped with poison. It may also poison the foe.
317: An attack that works only on a sleeping foe. It absorbs half the damage caused to heal the user's HP.
318: The user snares the foe with grass and trips it. The heavier the foe, the greater the damage.
319: The user enrages the foe into confusion. However, it also sharply raises the foe's Attack stat.
320: The user pecks the foe. If the foe is holding a Berry, the user plucks it and gains its effect.
321: After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.
322: The user makes a copy of itself using some of its HP. The copy serves as the user's decoy.
323: The user gathers all its light energy and releases it in one shot. It may also lower the foe's Sp. Def stat.
